task_completion.description: Verify that the 'field_observations' layer is selected or active for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'field_observations' layer is highlighted in the layer list or shown as the active layer for adding features.

task_completion.description: Verify that a point is placed at the approximate midpoint of the longer segment (Lima to Santiago).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point feature is added on the map along the western coast of South America, roughly halfway between Peru (Lima) and central Chile (Santiago), which is around northern Chile.

task_completion.description: Verify that the feature name is set to 'Rest_Stop'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Rest_Stop' is visible in the name or title field of the feature attribute form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the feature description includes 'Midpoint of longest leg'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Midpoint of longest leg' (or containing 'Midpoint') is visible in the description field of the feature attribute form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the new feature and changes are saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkmark or save button is clicked in the attribute form, and the new point is visible on the map interface.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the map GUI to create the feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the map interface, digitizing crosshairs, and attribute forms being used, rather than terminal commands or SQLite prompts.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the application to edit the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or database browser tools (like sqlite3) are used to run SQL INSERT commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target layer is 'field_observations'. The target feature name must contain 'Rest_Stop' and the description must contain 'Midpoint'. The segment from Lima to Santiago is longer than Quito to Lima, so the point should be placed roughly halfway between Lima and Santiago (around northern Chile). Note: Lima is not present in the dataset, so the agent must infer its location on the map.
